items.title: Resolve the reviewed under-$20K page release candidate
items.baseline: At commit c87e291, the already built and July 13 reviewed under-$20K candidate exists only as exact-path dirty work and is not part of repository history or the live site.
items.hypothesis: Capturing the unchanged, review-clean candidate in one exact-path commit will preserve the work and create a release-ready artifact without implying that it is already published or measured.
items.expected_effect: No traffic, indexing, or production effect before an authorized push and deployment; immediate effect is a reproducible reviewed commit for later release.
items.observation_window: Not started. If a later authorized release occurs, establish a URL-scoped observation window from the verified production deployment date.
items.decision: Capture the independently reviewed, QA-green candidate in one exact-path local commit. The later authorized production release is tracked separately as THC-DEP-001.
items.success_signal: The already reviewed page is either captured in one exact-path, QA-green commit or a concrete blocking defect is recorded without rebuilding the page.
items.gsc_evidence_classification: unavailable; configured collection mode is authenticated-browser-or-readonly-api, with collected-at and data-through dates UNKNOWN; this release-capture action does not require fresh GSC
qa_evidence: git diff and untracked-path whitespace checks passed
required site and deployment files are present
HTML and sitemap XML parse checks passed
the single inline script passed node --check
all ten local links and fragments resolved
blank, complete-$20K, over-budget, and invalid-negative calculator states passed
desktop 1440x900, mobile 390x844, and narrow 320x740 browser QA passed without horizontal overflow or clipped controls
independent review cycle 3 returned PASS with no P0-P3 findings after release-state and cycle-history evidence fixes
items.result: The review-clean candidate was captured locally in commit a90434e. The user granted release authorization on 2026-07-17; push, deployment, and production verification are now the pending release steps.
items.notes: The local capture is complete. Do not call the candidate live until THC-DEP-001 verifies the authorized push and production deployment.

items.title: Deploy the reviewed under-$20K guide and cloud measurement setup
items.success_signal: The reviewed guide and GSC infrastructure are on origin/main, Pages succeeds for the pushed SHA, and the live URL, sitemap, calculator states, and buyer-protective caveats pass production checks.
items.baseline: The guide is review-clean in local commit a90434e but is not on origin/main or production; unattended GSC collection is not installed in this repository.
items.hypothesis: A verified release plus fail-loud read-only GSC collection will make the guide measurable without weakening its buyer-protective claims.
items.expected_effect: The guide becomes live and enters a URL-scoped observation window; GSC collection becomes operational once the required GitHub secret and property access are configured.
items.observation_window: Start a 14-day URL-scoped window from the verified production deployment.
items.decision: Push the review-clean commits under the standing 2026-07-17 authorization and verify the pushed SHA, Pages run, production invariants, and release state.
items.production_verification: GitHub Pages run 29627141191 succeeded for the exact pushed SHA. The live guide and sitemap returned successfully and byte-matched the committed files; the sitemap includes the guide URL and the live page retains the reviewed cost/caveat invariants. GSC workflow run 29627800041 succeeded with the restricted service account and committed the first validated snapshot in 8ee6066.
items.result: The reviewed guide and unattended GSC infrastructure are on origin/main. The first real snapshot collected 20 impressions for the finalized 28-day window, confirmed a successful five-page sitemap, and found 5 of 6 priority URLs indexed.
items.notes: The under-$20K guide is the one not-yet-indexed priority URL in the first snapshot, which is expected immediately after release. Continue daily collection and use changed evidence rather than scan cadence to select work.

items.title: Repair LA and San Diego pages into answer-first local guides
items.baseline: The existing Los Angeles and San Diego source pages are cautious and official-source-backed, but they do not yet satisfy the mixed city intent shown by keyword and SERP research: buy/build, listings, communities/stays, and legal placement checks.
items.hypothesis: Repairing LA and San Diego into answer-first local guides will make them more useful for broad, uncertain city searchers while preserving legal caution.
items.expected_effect: Better buyer usefulness and clearer differentiation from vendor/state pages that overclaim; no guarantee of immediate traffic impact.
items.success_signal: LA and San Diego pages explain the broad local tiny-house landscape, distinguish listings/builders/communities/stays from legal placement, lead into official-source facts and next verification questions, and imply no parcel approval or legal permission.
qa_evidence: git diff --check and staged git diff --cached --check passed
required site and deployment files were present
Los Angeles and San Diego HTML parse checks passed
sitemap XML parse passed
local link check passed
research artifact size check passed
secret-pattern scan found only false positives on policy authorization text
node tools/seo-qa.mjs was unavailable because tools/seo-qa.mjs is not present in this checkout
items.production_verification: GitHub Pages run 29682037581 succeeded for commit 378bd659b042e96f97c6c0f5e8ae55436df6d84a. The live Los Angeles page, San Diego page, and sitemap returned HTTP 200 with last-modified 2026-07-19T09:42:08Z, and the live LA/SD pages showed the new human-first opening copy.
items.result: The LA and San Diego pages were repaired into answer-first local guides, reviewed independently with no P0-P2 findings, pushed to origin/main, deployed successfully, and verified in production.
items.notes: Addressable query research showed direct placement/rules exact demand is small, but broad and adjacent local demand is meaningful: San Diego broad 880, listings 1300, community 320, rentals 210, THOW 70; Los Angeles broad 720, listings 1600, community 170, rentals 140, ADU/THOW/laws around 10 each. The shipped repair preserves legal caution while adding buyer-facing listing, builder, community/stay, stop-condition, and next-verification guidance.

items.title: Scope national tiny-home build decision and builder due-diligence asset
items.baseline: General US tiny-home build demand is materially larger than exact local placement demand, but tiny-home build intent and ADU contractor intent are different markets.
items.hypothesis: A national build decision / builder due-diligence asset can serve broad tiny-home build demand without overclaiming legality or becoming a builder directory.
items.expected_effect: Clarifies whether the next product asset should address plans, kits, shells/trailers, builders, contractors, THOW builds, codes, permits, and risk checks.
items.success_signal: A reviewed brief defines a useful buyer-protection asset for people deciding whether to build, buy a kit/shell, or hire a tiny-home builder, with explicit separation from ADU-contractor demand.
qa_evidence: test -f site/.nojekyll passed
test -f .github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml passed
git diff --check passed
ops/seo-roadmap.json parsed with python3 -m json.tool
no site/** changes were present
independent read-only review returned PASS with no P0-P2 findings
items.evidence_classification: Durable July 18 DataForSEO build-intent research and completed THC-QUAL-001 page-quality handoff selected this brief. The July 20 GSC snapshot is fresh read-only monitoring evidence but is not causal support for this research action.
items.result: Scoped the national build decision / builder due-diligence asset as an internal brief only. The brief defines a buyer-protective decision guide comparing plans, kits, shells, trailers/THOWs, custom builders, conventional ADU contractors, and park model/RV-style products by fit, risk, written proof needed, and unresolved local verification.
items.notes: Do not target ADU-builder city pages, create a builder directory, recommend vendors, state legal/zoning conclusions, present typical or complete cost claims, create indexable pages, or start implementation from this completed brief action.

items.title: Build the national tiny-home build decision and due-diligence asset
items.baseline: The national build decision / builder due-diligence asset is scoped in an internal brief but not implemented or published.
items.hypothesis: A buyer-protective national decision guide can serve build, plans, kits, shells, trailer/THOW, builder, and code/permit research intent without becoming a builder directory or overclaiming legal placement.
items.expected_effect: Creates a useful review-clean asset for broad build-stage tiny-home searchers and strengthens the site beyond local placement pages.
items.success_signal: A future page or tool helps readers compare build paths, collect written proof, identify red flags, and verify local constraints without recommending vendors or implying legal/cost certainty.
items.notes: Future implementation requires a separate dispatch, exact paths, native QA, SERP/searcher POV review, and independent read-only review. No implementation was started by THC-BUILD-001.

items.title: Refresh indexing and early search evidence for the live support cluster
items.success_signal: A changed read-only snapshot identifies a discovery, indexing, or performance signal worth evaluating without requesting indexing.
items.notes: The first API snapshot is the baseline, not changed evidence. The 2026-07-19 snapshot is newer but only moved from 21 to 22 impressions with no clicks, sitemap change, unknown inspections, or indexing-state change; that is observation evidence, not a standalone project action. The authenticated browser review collected 2026-07-18 (report updated 2026-07-09) found only three expected protocol/www redirects, all correctly returning 301 to the HTTPS apex; see ops/gsc-indexing-review-2026-07-18.md. Do not request indexing or validate a fix for those aliases. Semrush is optional enrichment and must not block a GSC/public evidence refresh.

items.title: Choose the next buyer-protection tool from observed user and search evidence
items.success_signal: One bounded next tool or page is selected with evidence and explicit legal/source review gates.
items.notes: Do not scale location pages or add lead generation merely to maintain cadence. California demand research found broad/commercial demand but low fragmented exact placement demand; use targeted SERP/GSC evidence before choosing a California hub or additional city pages.
